executiveSummary.text: Regime is selective risk-off, not broad liquidation: S&P 500 -0.10%, Nasdaq -0.43%, Dow +0.35%, VIX -4.4% to 18.63; the stress is concentrated in AI duration, commodities and FX/rates, while small caps and Singapore held up.

Rates remain the valuation governor: 10Y/30Y eased to ~4.40%/4.86% intraday, but FRED 10Y real yield was still ~2.29% on Jun 23 and DXY rose to ~101.6. Lower oil helps inflation, but a stronger USD and hawkish Fed-rate repricing keep duration assets capped.

AI/semis had a positioning test into Micron earnings, not a thesis break: MU -0.3%, SOX -0.2% after the prior rout; TSM +1.0%, equipment mostly stable, but Microsoft -2.3% shows hyperscaler/AI-capex sensitivity spreading beyond chips.

Commodity sleeve took the abnormal hit: GLD -3.0%, SLV -7.1%, COPX -4.8%, URNM -2.5%; futures confirm gold -2.8%, silver -7.1%, copper -2.7%, Brent -5.1% as Hormuz/oil-risk premium unwound and USD/Fed-hike bets tightened financial conditions.
Singapore is a ballast today: STI ETF +0.4%, banks flat, REITs mostly firmer; Keppel DC REIT +1.8% and CICT +2.2% benefit from lower long yields, but USD strength is still a headwind for SGD assets.

Probability-weighted base case: 55% choppy consolidation/rotation, 25% renewed AI-led drawdown if Micron/PCE/Fed narrative worsens, 20% risk-on rebound if inflation data cools and semis confirm demand. Confidence: medium.

regime.label: Selective risk-off / rotation with rates still restrictive

usGlobal.what: US indices were mixed with tech lagging and Dow/small caps firmer.
usGlobal.why: Investors are rotating after the AI/chip gut-check; VIX easing argues against forced de-risking.
usGlobal.implication: Use weakness selectively; broad risk is not yet confirming a recessionary selloff.

usGlobal.what: Chip leadership is being tested around Micron earnings and AI spending scrutiny.
usGlobal.why: Recent news flow centers on AI capex anxiety and memory/HBM expectations rather than demand collapse.
usGlobal.implication: If MU validates HBM/datacenter demand, the selloff is positioning; if pricing/gross margins disappoint, multiple compression spreads to equipment and EDA.

ratesMacro.what: Nominal yields eased, but real yields remain high and USD firm.
ratesMacro.why: Oil risk premium unwound, but markets still price a hawkish Fed path into PCE/inflation risk.
ratesMacro.implication: Long-duration AI, REITs and gold need either lower real yields or lower USD to sustain rebounds.

ratesMacro.what: May PCE/inflation report is the next macro catalyst.
ratesMacro.why: Fresh Investopedia/market coverage expects the Fed-preferred inflation reading to test the high-for-longer narrative.
ratesMacro.implication: Cool PCE = relief in REITs/gold/AI multiples; hot PCE = USD up, real yields up, commodities and duration pressured.

asiaSingapore.what: Singapore market closed higher; STI ETF +0.44%, banks flat, REITs firmer.
asiaSingapore.why: Local market has low direct AI-hardware exposure and benefits from lower global long yields.
asiaSingapore.implication: SG dividend sleeve is doing its job as ballast; REIT beta improves only if rate relief persists.

asiaSingapore.why: Wide Fed-BoJ differential keeps carry alive while Tokyo warning risk caps further yen weakness.
asiaSingapore.implication: A disorderly yen reversal would tighten global liquidity and hit crowded risk/AI trades via carry unwind.

commodities.what: Oil fell sharply as Hormuz traffic/supply fears eased.
commodities.why: Fresh commodity coverage cites improving tanker movement and expectations of Iranian supply returning.
commodities.implication: 2nd order: lower oil reduces inflation impulse and helps consumers; 3rd order: lower inflation may help duration assets, unless USD strength dominates.

commodities.what: Precious and industrial metals sold off together.
commodities.why: This was a USD/real-yield/liquidity move plus oil-risk-premium unwind, not a simple safe-haven story.
commodities.implication: Scarcity thesis intact for copper/silver, but near-term path depends on real yields and China/global PMI confirmation.

geopolitics.what: Middle East oil-risk premium is being marked down.
geopolitics.why: Fresh reports cite easing Hormuz concerns and improved traffic; market transmission is via oil, inflation expectations, USD and rates.
geopolitics.implication: Good for inflation and margins, bad for crisis hedges; watch whether shipping insurance/freight actually normalizes.

geopolitics.what: China/geopolitical headlines are not the dominant price-setter today.
geopolitics.why: Current cross-asset moves are better explained by Fed/USD/AI positioning.
geopolitics.implication: Do not overfit China headlines unless they move copper, CNH, Asian credit or semicap order expectations.

moves.why: Global equity ETF caught broad USD/global risk repricing and non-US market timing effects.
moves.implication: Core compounding sleeve volatility; no thesis damage unless breadth/credit deteriorate.
moves.action: HOLD; add only on pre-set rebalance bands, invalidator = global earnings downturn plus credit stress.

moves.why: Commodities sold off as oil geopolitical premium unwound, USD rose and real yields stayed high.
moves.implication: Hits scarcity sleeve mark-to-market; thesis damage is low for copper/uranium supply bottlenecks, moderate for tactical precious metals while real yields stay elevated.
moves.action: HOLD/WATCH; add copper/uranium only on stabilization plus lower real yields or stronger China/AI-grid demand; invalidator = capex/order cuts and sustained dollar breakout.

moves.what: Microsoft -2.27% despite no single-stock threshold breach.
moves.why: Hyperscaler AI-capex scrutiny is spreading from chips to cloud platforms; MSFT is KL-relevant due big tech sleeve.
moves.implication: 2nd order: if capex ROI doubts rise, semis/equipment suffer before cloud cash machines; 3rd order: power/grid thesis may lag if datacenter buildouts are deferred.
moves.action: HOLD; add only if cloud growth/AI monetization remains intact after earnings, invalidator = capex up while margins and Azure growth disappoint.

moves.what: Singapore REITs firm: Keppel DC REIT +1.79%, CICT +2.16%.
moves.why: Lower long yields support rate-sensitive yield assets; local Singapore tape closed higher.
moves.implication: Supports KL dividend sleeve; still sensitive to USD strength and refinancing spreads.
moves.action: HOLD; add only to quality balance sheets if 10Y decisively breaks lower, invalidator = renewed rate spike or DPU cuts.

thesisImpact.what: AI scarcity thesis is bruised by positioning, not broken.
thesisImpact.why: TSM +1.02%, equipment mixed and MU flat after prior selloff; watch earnings/guidance rather than price alone.
thesisImpact.implication: If HBM and datacenter memory demand remain tight, selloff becomes add-on-weakness; if memory pricing rolls, reduce cyclicals first.
thesisImpact.action: WATCH/SELECTIVE ADD on confirmed demand and valuation reset.

thesisImpact.why: KL has SGD 664K cash at ~4%, giving dry powder while high real yields pressure duration/commodities.
thesisImpact.implication: Do not rush deployment into first downdraft; tranche into dislocations with thesis confirmation.

narratives.title: AI trade stress test shifts from chip supply to capex ROI
narratives.description: Market is asking whether hyperscaler spending earns acceptable returns. First-order pressure hits semis; second-order pressure hits equipment/EDA; third-order pressure can slow grid/power beneficiaries if datacenter construction is deferred.
narratives.priceImpact: negative short term, constructive long term if demand/guidance holds

narratives.title: Oil risk-premium unwind becomes a rates trade
narratives.description: Lower Brent reduces inflation expectations, but if USD rises because Fed-hike odds dominate, gold/metals can still fall. Transmission: oil down -> breakevens down -> nominal yields down; but USD/real yields up -> pressure commodities and long-duration equities.
narratives.source: Times of India/Reuters-derived commodity coverage, FXStreet, Yahoo quotes
narratives.priceImpact: mixed: disinflationary for equities, negative for commodity hedges

narratives.title: Singapore defensive profile visible
narratives.description: STI/banks/REITs are less exposed to AI hardware unwind. Lower yields help REITs, while banks need net-interest-margin resilience.
narratives.source: Business Times Singapore market coverage and Yahoo quotes

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil down is not automatically risk-on: it lowers headline inflation and supports consumers, but it also signals fading geopolitical premium and can coincide with USD strength; commodities suffer if real yields stay high.
secondOrderEffects.implication: For KL, keep GLD/SLV as diversification, not as a guaranteed crisis hedge; copper/uranium need demand confirmation.

secondOrderEffects.text: If Micron confirms strong HBM/datacenter demand, the market may rotate back into AI supply chain; if it misses, investors will question AI capex discipline across MSFT/AMZN/GOOG/META before cutting equipment multiples.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Watch guidance language and capex ROI, not only headline EPS.

secondOrderEffects.text: Yen intervention risk matters because carry trades fund global risk appetite. A sudden yen rally could force deleveraging in crowded winners even without a US macro shock.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Do not add aggressively to high-beta AI if USD/JPY breaks disorderly lower from intervention.

investment.thesis: Keep accumulating the AI infrastructure scarcity basket only on evidence-backed weakness.
investment.trigger: MU/HBM and hyperscaler earnings confirm datacenter demand while semicap names remain 10-15% below recent highs.
investment.whyNow: The market is stress-testing capex ROI; this creates opportunity only if demand is validated.
investment.invalidator: HBM pricing rolls over, hyperscalers cut capex, or equipment orders deteriorate for two quarters.

investment.thesis: Use Singapore banks/quality REITs as ballast, not growth engine.
investment.trigger: 10Y US yield sustains below 4.25% and SGD funding conditions stay benign.
investment.whyNow: REITs are rate-sensitive and are starting to respond to lower yields; banks remain dividend anchors.
investment.invalidator: Renewed yield spike, DPU cuts, or Singapore credit/property stress.

trading.thesis: Tactical rebound in commodities only after USD/real-yield pressure eases.
trading.trigger: DXY fails below 101 and 10Y real yield turns down for several sessions while GLD/SLV stop making lower lows.
trading.whyNow: Metals are oversold after abnormal moves, but catching falling knives before USD turns is low quality.
trading.invalidator: DXY breakout and real yields above recent highs.

trading.thesis: AI rebound trade is conditional on Micron/PCE.
trading.trigger: MU guidance confirms HBM tightness and PCE is not hot.
trading.whyNow: Positioning was hit hard; relief needs both micro and macro.
trading.invalidator: Hot PCE or weak AI-memory guidance.

watchlist.thesis: PCE/Fed repricing is the next cross-asset hinge.
watchlist.trigger: Core PCE materially above expectations or Fed speakers validate rate-hike risk.
watchlist.whyNow: Rates/real yields are driving USD, gold, REITs and AI multiples.
watchlist.invalidator: Benign inflation and lower real yields.

watchlist.thesis: Yen/carry stress can transmit into US tech.
watchlist.trigger: USD/JPY intervention headlines or sharp drop from 161-162 area.
watchlist.whyNow: The yen is near intervention-sensitive levels per fresh FX coverage.
watchlist.invalidator: Orderly yen stabilization without volatility spike.

riskWatch.name: Hot PCE / hawkish Fed repricing
riskWatch.description: Would lift real yields/USD and pressure AI duration, REITs and precious metals.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash ballast; avoid deploying into duration until real yields roll over.

riskWatch.name: AI capex ROI disappointment
riskWatch.description: If hyperscalers cannot show monetization, the selloff spreads from semis to equipment, EDA, grid/power and cloud.
riskWatch.mitigation: Prefer quality chokepoints; add only after guidance confirmation.

riskWatch.name: Yen carry unwind
riskWatch.description: USD/JPY near intervention zone; abrupt yen strength could force de-risking across crowded global trades.
riskWatch.mitigation: Avoid leverage; stagger buys; monitor VIX and USD/JPY together.

keyEvents.text: Micron Q3 2026 earnings/guidance expected after the close; market treating it as a key AI/HBM demand test.
May PCE/inflation report due Thursday; Fed-preferred inflation gauge may shape rate-hike odds.
Oil fell to multi-month lows as Hormuz traffic/supply fears eased; Brent -5.1%, WTI -4.6%.
keyEvents.source: Times of India/Reuters-derived coverage plus Yahoo quotes

keyEvents.text: Singapore stocks closed higher; STI +0.2% per Business Times, local tech relatively insulated from global AI hardware selloff.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The market hinge is whether lower oil becomes lower inflation/real yields, or whether hawkish Fed/USD strength dominates. For KL, the abnormal pain is commodities, while AI is in a thesis-verification window around MU/PCE/hyperscaler capex.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold the barbell. Do not chase first bounces. Use cash only in tranches when thesis data confirms: MU/HBM demand for semis, lower real yields for REITs/metals, stable USD/JPY for risk appetite.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore simplistic “geopolitical risk = gold up” narratives and one-day commodity panic. Also ignore portfolio-only noise unless it maps to rates, USD, AI demand or supply bottlenecks.
